title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the production memory-safety defense dossier contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for production memory-safety defense dossier after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Memory Incident Response
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 6594. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve first-failure artifacts, symbolize safely, contain exposure, reproduce, repair causally, and verify recovery and prevention.

Inspect the Memory Incident Response trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Memory Incident Response.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve first-failure artifacts, symbolize safely, contain exposure, reproduce, repair causally, and verify recovery and prevention. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Memory Incident Response, keep Memory Incident Response connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.
